details: A placeholder package containing only a package.json file with no executable code. The package declares a postinstall hook (node index.js) but ships no index.js — the script will fail with a module-not-found error upon install. This minimal package shape with a lifecycle script and no actual code is characteristic of a namespace-reservation pattern: the package name is claimed in advance for potential later use, often preceding delivery of a malicious payload in a subsequent version.
